column13: I always listen. However, if I am not convinced I do not immediately execute, if it is her primary physician  who is  requesting I inquire further.
Dr. Baz is very respectful to all the blood bank staff
Great leader and director; take care of his staff and always ready to help and support.
Highly committed to disciplined, credible, professional, transparent and respectful behavior with empathy, and no tolerance otherwise.
Appreciated and endeared as precious as a family member, blessed to have him as a director.
Sincerely, it is impossible to find a mentor and a director like Dr.  Araj.

Dr. Kassouf is a class act. Her attitude brings light and good spirit to the laboratory. Always smiling and cheerful. 
Way less anxious than I first got to know her and alot more laid back and relaxed. She is immensly helpful and always welcoming to suggestions and improvements.

Dr. Aoun might have the tendancy to drag on with her calls, as opposed to other attendings, for a longer period of time. 
On the other hand, whenever we reach out to her in regards to patient reports and follow ups, she is always helpful and responds immediately to inquiries.
She treats us like fellow colleagues which is something all techs appreciate.
She exudes humor and lightheartedness in her interactions with us.

I take great pride in having the opportunity to work under her guidance. 
Each year, I find myself growing and improving in my skills and knowledge, thank to her expert mentorship, encouraging leadership and support.
Dr. nancy is a great leader

Dr. Najla is everyone's favorite aunt. Always happy, cheerful, kind, warm and lovable. Sometimes she might seem like she has alot on her plate and anxious, but always finds the time to respond and help. Having her in the department is a breath of fresh air.
Dr. Jurdi is everything a tech would want in an attending pathologist: professional, kind, helpful, respectful and warm. The days when he is on call are always smooth and stress–free.

Dr. Shabb truly loves her job and the department and it shows through her energy and spirit. She might seem intimidating to some, however she always finds a way to light up the atmosphere with her witty and charming personality. Although contact with her is less frequent than her counterparts, she is always helpful and responsive.
My workspace is my second home, and the hospital staff are my family. I treat everyone with respect, empathy and care. I am extremely interested and dedicated to my work.

I am always available to discuss and manage any concern / inquiry for all our customers in a respectful and very efficient manner. 

I am a very client focused/dedicated Director who places the patients' and physicians' needs/concerns as my top priority and act accordingly.
Dr Mahfouz is a true leader.

Dr. Mahfouz consistently demonstrates professionalism and respect in all interactions. He is approachable, responsive and always available to support staff and clients. He shows a high commitment to patient care and team development.  His leadership fosters a collaborative environment.
Dr. Sinno is always available and ready to help. She always follows up with the lab. A true professional.

1– I was asked the other day by a staff about the results of a patient. I told that staff that patient results are confidential and that they will be directly shared with the requesting physician and the patient.
2–5–7–8– I mostly deal with Hemato–oncologists. I am sure many such as Dr. D.Noon. Dr. N.Moukalled, Dr. F.Kreidieh, Dr. I.AbouDalle, Dr. Bazarbachi (...etc) would gladly give many examples if asked.
He tends to give sarcastic comments or belittle some staff in front of others. He also gives unprofessional humor, and  sometimes directly criticizes staff unprofessionaly.
